---
title: "Bayesian Stochastic Volatility Modeling"
author: "Christopher Costello and Preston Sperduto"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  pdf: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```


library(quantmod)

# Example: load data
# Replace with your actual pipeline
getSymbols("SPY", from = "2010-01-01")
returns <- dailyReturn(Cl(SPY), type = "log")
returns <- na.omit(returns)

head(returns)


plot(returns, main = "Log Returns")

hist(returns, breaks = 50, main = "Histogram of Returns")


library(rugarch)

spec <- ugarchspec()
fit_garch <- ugarchfit(spec, data = returns)

garch_vol <- sigma(fit_garch)


plot(garch_vol, main = "GARCH Volatility")


library(rjags)
library(coda)

# Use your existing JAGS model + data prep here
# sv_fit <- ...

# Example placeholders:
# samples <- coda.samples(...)

# Trace plots
# plot(samples)

# ACF plots
# autocorr.plot(samples)

# sv_vol <- ...
# plot(sv_vol, main = "SV Volatility")

# Use your forecasting pipeline here
# Compare:
# - GARCH forecasts
# - SV forecasts

# Example placeholders:
# plot(...)

# Compute and display errors
# MSE, MAE, etc.
